replies.msg:
有用連結
CFM網站 - Lost and Found List及Shuttle班次 : https://cfm.cpce-polyu.edu.hk
玻璃房(小組研習室)Booking : https://mbs.cpce-polyu.edu.hk/ibooking/booking.php
校園設施Booking : https://sao.cpce-polyu.edu.hk/facilities-campus.php

Email：經常有重要嘢，記得留意Email，HKCC用全套Office 365，所以係Outlook同OneDrive而冇免費Google Drive
兩個校園附近極多巴士，而且好多你未必知，記得Check下

HKCC用語及設施一覽：
校園及交通
括號內指步行所需時間 (分鐘)
HHB：紅磡灣校園 - Science/Engine/IT/Design嘅Home Campus
最近港鐵站：黃埔(5)/紅磡(10)
鄰近巴士站：海韻軒(九巴稱紅樂道，設115特快去銅鑼灣、灣仔、中上環)
值得搭嘅巴士路線：門口就有115特快去銅鑼灣 灣仔 中上環、或到紅隧口大量往港島及九龍新界巴士

WK：西九龍校園，分南、北兩座，只有部份樓層南北相通 - Business/文科嘅Home Campus
最近港鐵站：油麻地(12-15)/奧運(10)
鄰近巴士站：柏景灣總站、西九龍站總站(10-12)、富榮花園、欣翔道(步程約10分鐘，有280X直入沙田、271X直入大埔)、佐敦道巴士站(步程約15分鐘，有970X直入西隧、E23直入東涌、260X直入屯門)
值得搭嘅巴士路線：W2、W3、914、城巴20(可轉乘A22往九龍灣、796C/796X往將軍澳或轉車站)同埋欣翔道或佐敦道巴士站上面提過嘅巴士

Shuttle：免費來往WK <> PolyU <> HHB之穿梭巴士，Express班次不經PolyU，時間表於CPCE CFM網站及Shuttle站，WK過海可善用Shuttle到紅隧口搭車

校園設施
想搵地方傾Project/食飯/做功課/Hea嘅話可以考慮下一啲周圍嘅枱，例如WK S7咁，除咗圓枱之外都有插座
圖書館(拉把)：
HKCC學生可以用PolyU本部嘅包玉剛圖書館 / WK / HHB 圖書館

WK - 入口及Counter @ N5/F
4樓：電腦房、圓枱、玻璃房
5樓：電腦、Printer、入口、借書還書借Reserve書Counter
6樓：藏書區、電腦、自修用座位、玻璃房(備有電腦及圓枱)

HHB - 入口及Counter @ 10樓
9樓：藏書區、電腦、自修用座位、玻璃房(備有電腦及圓枱)
10樓：電腦、Printer、入口、借書還書借Reserve書Counter、玻璃房(位於圖書館入口對面，長期開放)

^Exam Period嘅星期日及公眾假期，可以用貨Lift到WK 4樓溫書

學生電腦中心(網吧)：WK @ S5/F、HHB @ 11樓，提供電腦及Printer，並有Notebook及iPad租借服務，亦設有Printing Quota增值機

Print嘢
每一學年自動補充至2200 Quota，每個Quota增值要$0.1
Print每張黑白A4扣2個Quota，留意彩色Printer即使Print黑白照扣彩色價(15 Quota)
Express Printing Area設於WK S1/F、N7/F、S9/F，HHB 1樓、3樓、6樓，亦可到拉把/網吧Print

MPH/Hall(禮堂)： WK @ SUG/F、HHB @ 1樓，可以利用上面Link Book場
舞蹈室(鏡房)：WK @ NUG/F、HHB @ 2樓，可以利用上面Link Book場
明日：24小時開放，WK @ S2/F、HHB @ UG樓；備有叉電座嘅六角枱 - 實際上好嘈及好多MK長期霸佔，不宜傾Project
Pacific Coffee及7-ELEVEN：WK @ S3/F、HHB @ 4樓 - Pacific Coffee華潤國企不了，另外熱賣點會早啲收
學生保健中心(獸醫)：WK @ NLG/F (上9-13樓升降機隔離)、HHB @ LG/F ，$15睇1次醫生 - 盈健營運，喺其他盈健診所有優惠價，大約$170

網上資源
myHKCC：Add/Drop、睇自己時間表/GPA、睇Exam日期等
CARES @ myHKCC：用作報義工/Talk/課外活動之用及HM報Hotel Stay之用
Moodle：科目分發學科資源及交功課之用

學術類
Sem：Semester 學期，一學年最多有3個Sem
Sem 1：9至12月 - 13 Teaching Weeks
Sem 2：1至4月 - 13 Teaching Weeks
Summer Term：6至8月 - 7 Teaching Weeks
^Year 1 Sem1所有Tutorial都會係同同一班人一齊上，係識朋友嘅好機會

Lecture：教書堂，一般兩小時，部分科目達三小時
Tutorial：溫書堂，做Exercise/做Presentation/Review返Lecture講過嘅嘢，大概有25人1個Tutor
Lecturer：負責教你嗰科嘅老師
Mid-term：通常考返Week 1-6內容嘅測驗，佔Coursework嘅30-50%不等
Final：Final examination，佔整科0%/40%-50%
Sit堂：上其他lecturer/同一個lecturer其他時間嘅堂(通常Sit得都係唔得閒上自己個時間/ 你個lecturer太廢)
Day-off：星期一至五其中一日無堂上

Grade：每一科之成績，我哋有+冇-，A+ = 4.5、A = 4.0、B+ = 3.5、B = 3.0、C+ = 2.5、C = 2.0、D = 1.0、F = 0；並非所有科有派A+，例如英文
GPA：Grade Point Average，一個學期之所有grade加總/該學期科目總數
CGPA：Cumulative Grade Point Average，所有Grade加總除一共上過嘅科目總數
Credit：多數科係3個Credits；1個Sem大概讀15個credits，下限12、上限18
GE Subject：通識科目，所有學生須在5個類別科目中選讀至少4個類別之科
DS Subject：所屬Programme之下的必須科目
Reg科：每個sem 約Week 6開始，揀下一個sem之科目
Add/Drop：揀Timeslot/老師/科，每班限人數，經常出現0 Vacancy(空缺)，要鬥快Reg入去心儀班次，Sem頭開放Add/Drop，具體請留意Email及內附嘅Guide To Add/Drop
^Year 1 Sem 1冇得Reg科及Add/Drop

Transcript：成績表，可經myhkcc申請，每張$70
Turnitin：Check你份Assignment有冇抄人(Plagiarism)
Consultation Hour：每個路線每個禮拜總共有3小時在Office等你，可以問書、舉報freerider等等
Freerider：對份Project無/少貢獻嘅人，明明唔做嘢但係又好厚面皮咁痴住你去攞分
^如果想舉報freerider，請搜集好證據 (例如：WhatsApp Cap圖，Google Drive修改記錄等)
Killer：意指專出爛Grade之Lecturer

校園組織類及其他
CAT：Co-curriculum Achievements Transcript，活動紀錄(並非所有活動都會有CAT)；HKCC有獎學金比儲CAT的人
CFM校園設施管理辦事處：WK @ SLG/F、HHB @ LG/F，物品報失請往CFM
CSAO(CPCE Student Affairs Office)：處理學生活動成績表嘅Order，多數活動申請表都係呢度交；位於WK @ N3/F、HHB @ 4樓
FSCC(Further Studies and Career Centre)：入面有關於升學/搵工嘅資訊，有本薄仔有畢業生資料 (例如GPA+programme+interview details+佢收到咩offer)
IK(詢問處)：WK @ N2/F、HHB UG/F，主要喺度交文件
ITU：IT Unit，負責處理所有IT問題，Counter設於網吧門口
PDA(Personal Development Area)：由CSAO開設，可以搵到社工傾計。

寫信懶人包：（記住下款改返自己名）
To whom it may concern,

Due to the social instability in the early November 2019, HKCC cancelled all face-to-face teaching for the rest of the semester one and conducted online teaching. Moreover, due to the recent situation of the novel coronavirus infection, the school has also cancelled all face-to-face teaching and has conducted online teaching. Therefore, we, HKCC students, feel deeply worried about the effectiveness of learning under this teaching method and would like to request that HKCC improves current teaching methods and considers giving us a refund.

Since there is no face-to-face teaching, it causes a lot of difficulties in learning. Firstly, no instant interaction between students and teachers slows down many students’ learning pace. The time lag for receiving teachers’ feedback about the questions we would like to ask is a bit long. It is inconvenient for students to solve their academic questions. Hence, when students’ questions cannot be solved immediately, their doubts would be accumulated and then they might have difficulties in studying the subsequent chapters. Furthermore, some students claim some teachers who only told students to do self-study without uploading any online teaching videos and any extra academic learning materials to Moodle. However, every student has different learning abilities so some students cannot catch up with the progress without the lead of the teachers’ teaching and sufficient learning materials. Besides, students cannot make use of many learning services in school for efficient learning. As a result, not only the effectiveness of learning is reduced, but also students were unable to enjoy the facilities and services in the campus.

We sincerely hope that HKCC can understand our worries and will make some following changes for the sake of students’ academic performance and the fairness of the tuition fees: First, making online teaching and providing students with extra learning materials compulsory for every HKCC teacher from now on to facilitate students’ learning. Second, refunding part of the tuition fee in both semesters one and two because of the ineffective teaching methods and the lack of use of the campus facilities and services. Thank you.

Yours Sincerely,
XXX
Student of HKCC

衞生防護中心早前指，該名39歲男子本身有長期病患，1月29日起出現肌肉痛，同月31日發燒，入住伊利沙伯醫院，現時情況穩定，呼吸道樣本對新型冠狀病毒呈陽性反應。中心指，患者1月21日乘高鐵由本港往武漢（G306列車），並在2日後於長沙南站乘高鐵返港（G79列車）。中心初步調查顯示，患者潛伏期內沒有前往醫療機構、濕街市或海鮮市場，亦沒有接觸野生動物。

及

理工大學利用紅鸞道宿舍作中國學生隔離安排跟進】

今早居住於11期1座的39歲武漢肺炎患者不幸離世。我們理解區內傷心和惶恐的情緒，我們與民政署及社署聯絡提供可行的援助。死者的太太及子女正在鯉魚門渡假村接受隔離觀察。亦會再次聯絡衛生署尋找死者之兄長。

我們明白居民的擔憂，亦已促請相關部門在本日內向受影響的座數交代後續安排及家居處理的方法。

另外，我們今日在理工學生會得悉理工大學有意用紅鸞道的宿舍作中國學生的隔離設施，並只有出入探熱，此安排並無隔離作用之餘亦對周邊社區有影響，我們正聯絡理工大學了解，及反映憂慮。

圖書館(拉把)：

WK - 入口及Counter @ N5/F
4樓：電腦房、圓枱、玻璃房
5樓：電腦、Printer、入口、借書還書借Reserve書Counter
6樓：藏書區、電腦、自修用座位、玻璃房(備有電腦及圓枱)

^Exam Period嘅星期日及公眾假期，可以用貨Lift到WK 4樓溫書

學生電腦中心(網吧)：WK @ S5/F、HHB @ 11樓，提供電腦及Printer，並有Notebook及iPad租借服務，亦設有Printing Quota增值機

Print嘢
每一學年自動補充至2200 Quota，每個Quota增值要$0.1
Print每張黑白A4扣2個Quota，留意彩色Printer即使Print黑白照扣彩色價(15 Quota)
Express Printing Area設於WK S1/F、N7/F、S9/F，HHB 1樓、3樓、6樓，亦可到拉把/網吧Print

MPH/Hall(禮堂)： WK @ SUG/F、HHB @ 1樓，可以利用上面Link Book場
舞蹈室(鏡房)：WK @ NUG/F、HHB @ 2樓，可以利用上面Link Book場
明日：24小時開放，WK @ S2/F、HHB @ UG樓；備有叉電座嘅六角枱 - 實際上好嘈及好多MK長期霸佔，不宜傾Project
Pacific Coffee及7-ELEVEN：WK @ S3/F、HHB @ 4樓 - Pacific Coffee華潤國企不了，另外熱賣點會早啲收
學生保健中心(獸醫)：WK @ NLG/F (上9-13樓升降機隔離)、HHB @ LG/F ，$15睇1次醫生 - 盈健營運，喺其他盈健診所有優惠價，大約$170

To whom it may concern,

Due to the social instability in the early November 2019, HKCC cancelled all face-to-face teaching for the rest of the semester one and conducted online teaching. Moreover, due to the recent situation of the novel coronavirus infection, the school has also cancelled all face-to-face teaching and has conducted online teaching. Therefore, we, HKCC students, feel deeply worried about the effectiveness of learning under this teaching method and would like to request that HKCC improves current teaching methods and considers giving us a refund.

Since there is no face-to-face teaching, it causes a lot of difficulties in learning. Firstly, no instant interaction between students and teachers slows down many students’ learning pace. The time lag for receiving teachers’ feedback about the questions we would like to ask is a bit long. It is inconvenient for students to solve their academic questions. Hence, when students’ questions cannot be solved immediately, their doubts would be accumulated and then they might have difficulties in studying the subsequent chapters. Furthermore, some students claim some teachers who only told students to do self-study without uploading any online teaching videos and any extra academic learning materials to Moodle. However, every student has different learning abilities so some students cannot catch up with the progress without the lead of the teachers’ teaching and sufficient learning materials. Besides, students cannot make use of many learning services in school for efficient learning. As a result, not only the effectiveness of learning is reduced, but also students were unable to enjoy the facilities and services in the campus.

We sincerely hope that HKCC can understand our worries and will make some following changes for the sake of students’ academic performance and the fairness of the tuition fees: First, making online teaching and providing students with extra learning materials compulsory for every HKCC teacher from now on to facilitate students’ learning. Second, refunding part of the tuition fee in both semesters one and two because of the ineffective teaching methods and the lack of use of the campus facilities and services. Thank you.

Yours Sincerely,
XXX
Student of HKCC

Thanks for writing to the College, and we can understand your concerns.  Please let me share with you what the College has considered before deciding on the current measures to address the challenges we are all facing.

 

What we are all facing right now is an emergency – a fast-spreading epidemic on a global scale, with potentially fatal consequences. Given so, the College has no choice but to suspend face-to-face classes temporarily, so as to protect the health and safety of students and staff alike. And we need to resort to online teaching and learning as an interim measure, so that students can progress in their study and/or to graduate without serious delay.

 

Once the threats of the epidemic are behind us, we’ll resume face-to-face teaching.  In the meantime, the College is taking a number of steps to support quality online teaching and learning:

 

·       Online teaching materials (e.g. PowerPoint slides with voice-over) will be uploaded before class, so that you can get prepared beforehand;

·       Your lecturers will interact in real time with you during the scheduled class time as well as consultation hours (according to the College timetable) through digital means such as Moodle Forum, email, etc. During those hours, you can ask questions and get your lecturers’ feedback immediately;

·       Extra learning materials (e.g., on-line exercises and quizzes) will be prepared by your lecturers to gauge your learning progress, and additional help will be offered to you where needed. 

 

In addition, more sophisticated software, such as Microsoft Teams, will be utilised in the near future to improve the interactivity and effectiveness of online teaching.  In fact, such development is not restricted to answering the pressing needs of the moment but has far-reaching consequences for all of us, since blended learning (face-to-face learning coupled with online learning) is a growing trend in tertiary education worldwide.   

 

Coupling with these new measures, the College has extended the period of dropping a subject until 7 March 2020.  You can choose to drop a subject on or before 7 March and have your paid tuition fee refunded afterwards, and the subject record will not appear on your transcript.

 

Let’s hope that the epidemic will soon be behind us and we can resume face-to-face teaching.  In the meantime, please be reassured that the College will do all it can to help students learn effectively amid the limitations posed by the challenging circumstances.

To whom it may concern,

Due to the novel coronavirus spreading the community, HKCC has decided to cancel all the face-to-face teaching till 2 March 2020 up to this stage, while e-learning will start from 9 February.

The development of the virus is unpredictable and there is possibility that the face-to-face classes will still suspend over the whole semester. Some subjects have some announcements on the teaching and assignment arrangements during this period. However, several subjects require students to form groups to complete the group assignments. I personally believe such arrangement is not appropriate.

In usual learning, students are allowed to interact with classmates during lectures and tutorials. So, students are able to find some groupmates to work on the group assignments together. However, with the online teaching method, students are hard to find some groupmates. So, the formation of groups is hardly to be done. Some lecturers said if students are able to form groups, they will take the responsibility to form the groups by random draw. This is extremely unfair as students are not allowed to choose the groupmates by their choice.
Moreover, all citizens are encouraged to stay at home during this challenging period and I afraid that students cannot discuss the assignments in an interactive and effective manner. Even though we can discuss the assignments through digital means, the quality is a major concern.

Given the above worries on the arrangements on group assignments, I sincerely hope that the college will try to inform the lecturers to use other formats of assessment to replace group assignments, such as changing to individual assignment(s). Thank you.

Yours truly,
Xxxxx
Student of HKCC
